senses.definition: the first meal we have in the early hours of the day

examples.x: Breakfast plays an important role as it provides the body with the necessary energy and nutrients to start the day.
He had a toasted English muffin with scrambled eggs and a side of crispy bacon for breakfast.
She enjoyed a bowl of warm oatmeal topped with sliced bananas for breakfast.
The café served a delicious breakfast sandwich with eggs, cheese, and ham on a toasted bagel.
The children enjoyed a bowl of chocolate cereal with cold milk and a glass of orange juice for breakfast.

senses.definition: a meal that is eaten after the regular breakfast, typically as a mid-morning snack or additional meal
a substantial and hearty meal traditionally eaten in the morning
a meal that is consumed during the late-night hours, typically after midnight
a type of breakfast food or beverage that is designed to be quickly and easily prepared, often in a powdered or liquid form
a meal that includes champagne or other sparkling wine, often consumed in the morning or as part of a celebratory occasion
a light midday meal, typically lunch
a meal served late in the morning, as a combination of breakfast and lunch

examples.x: Many restaurants offer bottomless mimosas or bloody marys as part of their brunch menu to accompany the food.
The brunch buffet included both savory and sweet options, satisfying everyone's tastes and preferences.
Hosting a brunch at home can be a delightful way to entertain guests, with dishes prepared ahead of time for easy serving and enjoyment.

senses.definition: a light refreshment or snack, typically enjoyed around 11 a.m., often consisting of tea or coffee accompanied by biscuits, pastries, or similar small treats
a meal we eat in the middle of the day

examples.x: I made a Greek salad with feta cheese and olives for a healthy and flavorful lunch.
Sarah and her friend had a bowl of pasta with marinara sauce and a side of garlic bread for lunch.
She packed a lunchbox with a turkey wrap, carrot sticks, and a yogurt cup for a balanced lunch.
Some people choose to skip lunch when they are busy or not feeling hungry.
The café served a delicious lunch special of grilled salmon with roasted vegetables.

senses.definition: the food such as fruit, sandwiches, etc. taken to work or school
a promotional offer or package from a restaurant, cafe, or food establishment that includes a combination of food items or dishes
a light meal with tea including sandwiches and pastries, traditionally served in the afternoon
a small meal that consists of cakes and tea, eaten in the afternoon
a meal that includes cakes, bread and butter with tea, eaten in the early evening
the main meal of the day that we usually eat in the evening

examples.x: For dinner, I cooked a delicious chicken stir-fry with vegetables.
Gathering for dinner together as a family is a cherished tradition in our household.
They grilled hamburgers and hot dogs for a casual summer dinner.
They had a family dinner with a roasted chicken, rice, and steamed broccoli.
We ordered takeout pizza for an easy dinner.

senses.definition: a social gathering where guests are invited to a host's home for an evening meal, often accompanied by drinks and conversation
a formal or elaborate meal that consists of multiple courses
a meal eaten in the evening, typically lighter than dinner and often the last meal of the day

examples.x: They gathered around the table for a family supper of soup and sandwiches.
She prepared a hearty supper of roast chicken with vegetables and mashed potatoes.
He enjoyed a light supper of salad and grilled fish after a long day at work.
The cafe offers a selection of soups and sandwiches for those looking for a quick supper option.

senses.definition: a late-night meal or late-night dining culture in Hong Kong, where people gather to eat and socialize after midnight
a small meal or snack that is consumed during the late-night hours, typically after dinner and before bedtime
a meal that is served on board an aircraft during a flight
a large and formal meal for many people, often for a special event

examples.x: The wedding banquet featured multiple courses of gourmet cuisine served to guests seated at elegantly decorated tables.
The annual company banquet celebrated the team's achievements over the past year with speeches, awards, and a lavish dinner.
The royal banquet was held in the grand hall of the palace, where dignitaries and guests enjoyed exquisite food and entertainment.
A traditional Chinese banquet typically includes a variety of dishes symbolizing prosperity, happiness, and longevity.
The charity banquet raised funds for a local cause, bringing together donors and supporters for an evening of philanthropy and camaraderie.

senses.definition: an outdoor party during which food, such as meat, fish, etc. is cooked on a metal frame over an open fire

examples.x: During summer, we often enjoy a family barbecue at the park.
He invited me to his barbecue next Saturday.
The barbecue is starting at 3 pm, don’t forget to bring your appetite.
We had a barbecue in our backyard last weekend.
We're planning a barbecue in the backyard this weekend with friends and family.

senses.definition: a special discounted meal or menu item offered for a limited time
a meal with many dishes from which people serve themselves at a table and then eat elsewhere

examples.x: The hotel offered a lavish buffet with a wide variety of international cuisines.
Guests enjoyed the seafood buffet, featuring fresh oysters, shrimp, and crab legs.
The company organized a buffet lunch for its employees during the conference.
The buffet included a salad bar, hot entrees, and a dessert station.
We sat at a table near the window to enjoy our buffet breakfast with a view of the garden.

senses.definition: a light and informal selection of food items served between meals
fancy and carefully prepared food with beautiful presentation and top-notch ingredients
a traditional Japanese multi-course meal that showcases seasonal and regional ingredients
the meal that people eat during an outing in nature, typically in a park or on a beach
a meal or party where each guest brings a dish to share with the group, typically without prior coordination of what dishes will be prepared
a pre-prepared and cooked meal that only needs heating before being ready to be eaten
a shared meal that is prepared and consumed by a group of people, often within a family or close-knit community
a discounted combo meal with a main dish, sides, and a beverage offered at fast food restaurants
the pre-dawn meal consumed by Muslims during the Islamic fasting month of Ramadan
the evening meal that Muslims consume to break their fast during the Islamic month of Ramadan
a small amount of food that is taken into the mouth at one time, often to chew or swallow

examples.x: She took a bite of the apple and savored its sweetness.
He offered me a bite of his sandwich to taste the new flavor.
The child nibbled on a bite of the cookie, enjoying the treat.
After a long day, a hot bite of pizza was just what she needed.
They shared bites of dessert, each trying different flavors.

senses.definition: a meal that is complete and satisfying

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, I was famished and enjoyed a square meal of grilled chicken, rice, and vegetables.
The restaurant's Sunday brunch offers a square meal with a variety of breakfast and lunch options.
In my grandmother's house, you could always count on a square meal, complete with a meat dish, vegetables, and homemade bread.
When you're out camping, a square meal by the campfire can be a comforting experience.
The school cafeteria has been working to provide students with healthier and more nutritious square meals.

senses.definition: a light morning meal that typically includes items such as bread, fruits, and coffee, with a focus on simplicity and ease of preparation
an informal outdoor event where the food is often grilled or barbecued and prepared to be enjoyed in a social gathering
a meal that consists of jam, cream, and tea with small cakes, eaten in the afternoon
a traditional British morning meal with eggs, bacon, sausage, beans, tomatoes, black pudding, and toast or fried bread, usually served with tea or coffee
a meal with fine food or a large meal for many people celebrating a special event

examples.x: The Thanksgiving feast included roast turkey, stuffing, cranberry sauce, and pumpkin pie, enjoyed by family and friends gathered around the table.
The medieval feast recreated historical dishes and entertainment, transporting guests back in time to experience royal dining.
The village feast celebrated the harvest season with a communal gathering, featuring locally sourced food and traditional music.
The annual cultural festival concluded with a feast showcasing dishes from around the world, highlighting diversity and culinary traditions.
The birthday feast was a grand affair, with a variety of dishes prepared to delight the honored guests and mark the occasion joyfully.

senses.definition: a midday meal or gathering where alcoholic beverages, such as cocktails or wine, are consumed instead of solid food
a midday meal that is typically more formal than a light lunch and is often served in a social or business setting
a big meal
the food that we eat regularly during different times of day, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner

examples.x: I cooked a delicious meal of grilled chicken with roasted vegetables.
On average, people consume three meals per day: breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
She ordered a takeaway meal of pizza and garlic bread for dinner.
She planned a romantic candlelit meal for their anniversary.
The meal was served buffet-style with a variety of dishes to choose from.

senses.definition: a meal that is pre-cooked and only needs reheating before it is ready to be eaten
a buffet-style meal with a variety of cold and hot dishes
a small meal that is usually eaten between the main meals or when there is not much time for cooking

examples.x: At the party, there were many snacks to choose from.
He keeps some snacks in his desk drawer at work.
Potato chips are a common snack, but they aren't very healthy.
She often has a snack between breakfast and lunch to stave off hunger.
She packed a healthy snack of fruit and yogurt for work.

senses.definition: a feast featuring a variety of delicious dishes that bring immense satisfaction and pleasure to those partaking in it
a meal bought from a restaurant or store to be eaten somewhere else

examples.x: We decided to get takeaway for dinner instead of cooking tonight.
The takeaway was delivered right to our doorstep in less than 30 minutes.
After a long day, nothing beats a hot takeaway from our favorite Indian restaurant.
We’re having takeaway tonight because I don’t feel like cooking.
The best takeaway I’ve had in years was from a local sushi place.

senses.definition: a meal bought from a restaurant or store to be eaten somewhere else

examples.x: They decided to order takeout for dinner since they did not feel like cooking.
The convenience of takeout allowed them to enjoy a delicious meal without the hassle of cooking.
The takeout from their favorite Chinese restaurant arrived quickly and was still hot.

senses.definition: food that is bought from a restaurant, etc. to be eaten elsewhere

examples.x: She ordered a sandwich and a coffee to-go so she could eat during her lunch break.
The restaurant offers a convenient to-go option for those who prefer to enjoy their meals at home.
The café has a special line for customers who want to order their drinks to-go instead of dining in.
He forgot to bring his to-go cup for coffee, so he had to drink it quickly at the counter.

senses.definition: a meal or reception held after a wedding ceremony, typically consisting of a formal sit-down meal with multiple courses
a meal that is provided by a restaurant, catering service, or other food establishment to its employees or staff
